 Compendium
 (The Codex)
 The Authoritative Reference for Owners, Concierges, and the Curious
 Curated under the guidance of Magnus, the Auto-Alchemist
 Edition VII — Revised Spring 2026
 Alchemy Auto · Founded MMIX · Sedona, Arizona

The Alchemy Auto Codex  ·  Page 2
Table of Contents
Chapter 1 — The History & Philosophy of Alchemy Auto
Chapter 2 — The Aurelius EV Sedan
Chapter 3 — The Mercurial GT
Chapter 4 — The Vulcan Electric Truck
Chapter 5 — The Nimbus Hybrid SUV

The Alchemy Auto Codex  ·  Page 3
Chapter 1: The History & Philosophy of Alchemy
Auto
Alchemy Auto was founded on March 14, 2009, in Sedona, Arizona, by Dr. Helena Voss-Aurelio, a
former materials scientist who left a tenured chair in electrochemistry to pursue what she called “the
transmutation of motion.” The company's founding charter, signed in a converted copper smelter that
still serves as the lobby of the Sedona headquarters, declares a single mission: to turn base
transportation into something precious. The founder's signature still appears, embossed in gold leaf,
on the build plaque of every vehicle the company produces.
The brand name is no accident. Voss-Aurelio held that the automobile of the twentieth century was
lead — heavy, toxic, and common — and that the task of the twenty-first was to make it gold. The
corporate motto, “Solve et Coagula” (“dissolve and recombine”), is stamped beneath the hood of
every Alchemy Auto vehicle and appears on the company seal alongside the founding year, rendered
in Roman numerals as MMIX.
The Transmutation Philosophy
Internally, Alchemy Auto's design doctrine is known simply as “The Transmutation.” It rests on three
stages borrowed from classical alchemy: Nigredo (the blackening, or honest assessment of what is
wrong with a design), Albedo (the whitening, or purification toward simplicity), and Rubedo (the
reddening, or the final ennobling touch). Every model program must formally pass through all three
gates, chaired by a panel called the College of Adepts, before it earns a production signature. The
Aurelius program, for example, spent 19 months in Nigredo alone.

Every model program must formally pass through all three
gates, chaired by a panel called the College of Adepts, before it earns a production signature. The
Aurelius program, for example, spent 19 months in Nigredo alone.
The Four-Element Brand Pillars
The brand is organized around four elemental pillars, each governing a domain of the ownership
experience: Earth governs durability and materials (the company's 12-year corrosion philosophy
descends from this pillar); Water governs comfort, ride, and the flow of the cabin experience; Fire
governs performance, propulsion, and energy; and Air governs software, connectivity, and the
over-the-air spirit that keeps a vehicle current. Each Alchemy Auto vehicle carries a small
four-element rosette on the C-pillar, with the dominant element for that model rendered slightly larger
— Fire is enlarged on the Mercurial GT, Air on the Philosopher.
The first vehicle, an experimental roadster called the Prima Materia, never reached production; only
three were hand-built between 2009 and 2011. The company's first production vehicle arrived in
2014, and by 2026 Alchemy Auto offers exactly five models, a deliberately small line-up the founder
described as “five perfect transmutations rather than fifty compromises.” The five are the Aurelius, the
Mercurial GT, the Vulcan, the Nimbus, and the Philosopher.
The Sedona Headquarters & Manufacturing
Alchemy Auto's spiritual and corporate home remains the converted copper smelter on the northern
edge of Sedona, a 38-acre campus the company calls the “Athanor” after the alchemist's furnace.

The Alchemy Auto Codex  ·  Page 4
The campus houses the design studio, the College of Adepts chambers, the brand museum, the
flagship Philosopher's Lounge, and the low-volume hand-assembly hall where the Mercurial GT and
the Philosopher are built. Higher-volume production is split across two Arizona sites: the Flagstaff
Atelier, opened in 2017, builds the Aurelius and the Nimbus, while the Mesa Forge, opened in 2022,
builds the Vulcan exclusively. The company built its 250,000th vehicle in January 2026.
The founder, Dr. Helena Voss-Aurelio, served as chief executive until 2021 and remains chair of the
College of Adepts and the company's largest individual shareholder. Day-to-day leadership passed in
2021 to chief executive Idris Calloway, formerly the head of the Fire pillar, while the founder's
daughter, Dr. Mira Voss, leads battery and materials research. The company employs roughly 9,400
people across the three Arizona campuses and a design satellite in Turin, Italy.
The Codex & the Concierge
This Compendium — the Codex — is the canonical reference for every fact about the marque, and it
is the knowledge source from which the brand's AI concierge, Magnus, the Auto-Alchemist, draws its
answers. Magnus is named for the medieval scholar Albertus Magnus and is embodied both in the
in-car assistant and in the Owner's Club application. When an owner asks Magnus a question about a
specification, an interval, a warranty term, or a Club benefit, the answer is grounded in the chapters
that follow. The Codex is revised twice yearly; this is Edition VII, current as of Spring 2026.
Chapter 2: The Aurelius EV Sedan
The Aurelius is Alchemy Auto's electric luxury sedan and the volume heart of the brand. Named for
the philosopher-emperor Marcus Aurelius, it is built on the company's second-generation Solar
skateboard platform and assembled at the Flagstaff Atelier.

Named for
the philosopher-emperor Marcus Aurelius, it is built on the company's second-generation Solar
skateboard platform and assembled at the Flagstaff Atelier. It seats five and competes directly
against the established executive electric sedans, but distinguishes itself through the company's
signature gold-anodized accents and its “Stoic Mode” minimalist driving interface.
Trims, Powertrains & Pricing
The Aurelius is offered in three trims: Base, Lumen, and the flagship Magnum Opus. The Base is
rear-wheel drive and produces 355 horsepower with 380 lb-ft of torque, reaching 60 mph in 4.9
seconds. The Lumen adds a front motor for all-wheel drive, producing 442 horsepower and 488 lb-ft,
with a 0–60 mph time of 3.8 seconds. The Magnum Opus produces 528 horsepower and 561 lb-ft
of torque, reaching 60 mph in just 3.1 seconds. Starting prices are $62,400 for the Base, $74,900
for the Lumen, and $91,500 for the Magnum Opus.

The Alchemy Auto Codex  ·  Page 5
Battery, Range & Charging
The Base and Lumen use an 84.5 kWh usable lithium-iron-phosphate (LFP) battery, while the
Magnum Opus uses a larger 104 kWh nickel-manganese-cobalt (NMC) pack. The Base trim
delivers an EPA-estimated 358 miles of range; the Lumen is rated at 341 miles, and the Magnum
Opus at 372 miles despite its added power. All Aurelius models accept up to 11 kW of AC Level 2
charging. DC fast charging peaks at 210 kW on the LFP cars and 250 kW on the Magnum Opus,
enabling a 10–80 percent charge in 26 minutes (LFP) or 22 minutes (NMC) under ideal conditions.
The Aurelius uses the North American Charging Standard (NACS) port.
Dimensions, Colors & Technology
The Aurelius measures 196.1 inches long, 76.4 inches wide, and 56.2 inches tall, on a 116.8-inch
wheelbase, with a curb weight ranging from 4,710 to 5,090 pounds. It offers 15.2 cubic feet of trunk
space plus a 2.9 cubic-foot frunk. Standard exterior colors are Quicksilver Silver, Nigredo Black, and
Albedo White; premium finishes (a $1,950 option) include Rubedo Red, Cinnabar Orange, and the
signature Aurum Gold. The cabin centers on a 17.1-inch central touchscreen running the Air-pillar
“Aether OS,” a 12.3-inch driver display, a 22-speaker Helios audio system on Lumen and above, and
standard over-the-air updates. The Magnum Opus adds the “Empyrean” glass roof with
electrochromic dimming and rear-axle steering.

The Magnum Opus adds the “Empyrean” glass roof with
electrochromic dimming and rear-axle steering.
Driving Modes & Stoic Mode
The Aurelius offers four drive modes named for the four brand pillars: Earth (maximum efficiency and
range), Water (the default comfort tune), Fire (sharpened throttle, firmer dampers, and on the
Magnum Opus a launch-control function), and Air (a low-drag highway mode that raises regenerative
coasting). Overlaid on these is “Stoic Mode,” a signature interface that dims all non-essential displays
to a single speed readout and a monochrome map, intended to reduce distraction. One-pedal driving
is standard with three regeneration levels, and the Magnum Opus adds an adaptive air suspension
with a 1.6-inch range of ride-height adjustment.
Warranty Snapshot & Service Notes
Like all Alchemy EVs, the Aurelius carries the 4-year / 50,000-mile bumper-to-bumper warranty and
the 8-year / 100,000-mile high-voltage battery warranty detailed in Chapter 8. Routine service follows
the EV schedule in Chapter 7: tire rotation every 7,500 miles, cabin filter every 24,000 miles, and a
battery-coolant change at 100,000 miles. The Aurelius ships with the Hearth home wall connector
described in Chapter 9 and includes two years of complimentary scheduled maintenance on the
Lumen and Magnum Opus trims.
Chapter 3: The Mercurial GT
The Mercurial GT is Alchemy Auto's two-door sports coupe and the brand's clearest expression of the
Fire pillar. Unusually for the company, it retains internal combustion — offered as both a pure-petrol
and a performance-hybrid — reflecting the founder's belief that the company must “master fire before

The Alchemy Auto Codex  ·  Page 6
abandoning it.” It is hand-finished at the Sedona headquarters in batches of no more than 40 cars per
week.
Engine Options & Performance
Two powertrains are offered. The Mercurial GT uses a 3.0-liter twin-turbocharged inline-six
producing 473 horsepower and 442 lb-ft of torque, driving the rear wheels through an 8-speed
dual-clutch transmission; it reaches 60 mph in 3.9 seconds and tops out at 174 mph. The Mercurial
GT Sigma pairs the same engine with a front-axle electric motor for a combined 671 horsepower and
553 lb-ft, all-wheel drive, a 0–60 time of 2.9 seconds, and a top speed of 196 mph. The Sigma's 14.2
kWh battery allows up to 31 miles of electric-only running and recharges at up to 50 kW DC.

Chassis, Trims & Pricing
The Mercurial GT starts at $98,750 and the GT Sigma at $132,000. A no-cost “Track Adept” package
and a $9,400 “Cinnabar Carbon” package (carbon-ceramic brakes, carbon roof, and forged 21-inch
wheels) are available on both. The car rides on a double-wishbone front and multi-link rear
suspension with adaptive dampers; the Sigma adds active anti-roll bars and a torque-vectoring rear
differential. Fuel economy for the petrol GT is EPA-rated at 21 mpg combined, while the Sigma
achieves 24 mpg combined plus 31 miles of electric range. The Mercurial GT seats two, weighs 3,640
pounds (3,985 for the Sigma), and offers 9.1 cubic feet of trunk space.

The Mercurial GT seats two, weighs 3,640
pounds (3,985 for the Sigma), and offers 9.1 cubic feet of trunk space.
Heritage, Colors & Interior
The Mercurial GT debuted in 2018 as the model that proved Alchemy Auto could build a driver's car,
and it remains the only model wearing an enlarged Fire rosette on the C-pillar. Signature exterior
finishes are Quicksilver Silver, Mercury Liquid (a mirror-effect paint, a $3,400 option), Cinnabar Red,
and Nigredo Black. The cabin seats two in a 2+0 layout with available hand-stitched Volterra leather,
a 12.3-inch driver display, a 10.25-inch central screen running Aether OS, and a frameless interior
mirror. A removable carbon hardtop is available on the GT Sigma, turning it into a targa.
Maintenance Notes
As a combustion model, the Mercurial GT follows the petrol/hybrid schedule in Chapter 7: 5W-40
full-synthetic oil and filter every 10,000 miles or 12 months (7.4-quart capacity), spark plugs at 45,000
miles, and dual-clutch transmission fluid at 60,000 miles. The carbon-ceramic brakes on the Cinnabar
Carbon package are rated for the life of the vehicle under normal road use. The GT Sigma's 14.2 kWh
hybrid battery is covered under the same 8-year / 100,000-mile high-voltage warranty as the brand's
full EVs.

The Alchemy Auto Codex  ·  Page 7
Chapter 4: The Vulcan Electric Truck
The Vulcan is Alchemy Auto's all-electric full-size pickup, named for the smith-god of the forge. Built
on the Anvil body-on-frame-meets-skateboard architecture, it is the company's most utilitarian vehicle
and the only one assembled at the dedicated Mesa Forge plant. It is offered in two configurations: the
dual-motor Vulcan Forge and the quad-motor Vulcan Cyclops.
Power, Range & Capability
The Vulcan Forge produces 611 horsepower and 685 lb-ft from two motors and reaches 60 mph in
4.3 seconds. The Vulcan Cyclops uses four motors for 824 horsepower and 938 lb-ft, hitting 60 mph
in 3.4 seconds despite weighing 6,780 pounds. The Vulcan Forge is rated to tow 11,000 pounds
and carry a maximum payload of 2,180 pounds; the Cyclops tows up to 13,500 pounds. Both use
a 165 kWh battery; the Forge is EPA-rated at 324 miles of range and the Cyclops at 301 miles. DC
fast charging peaks at 270 kW, enabling a 10–80 percent charge in about 32 minutes.
Bed, Frunk & Storage
The Vulcan's steel-and-composite bed measures 68.5 inches long, 51.0 inches wide, and 21.0 inches
deep, with 62.3 cubic feet of volume and 50.4 inches between the wheel housings — wide enough for
standard building sheets laid flat. The front trunk (frunk) offers 11.1 cubic feet of lockable,
drainable storage and a 400-pound load rating. The truck provides eleven 120-volt outlets and two
240-volt outlets, delivering up to 11.5 kW of exportable power through the “Forge Power” system —
enough to run a job site or power a home for roughly three days. A motorized tonneau cover and an
integrated 60-quart cooler in the bed wall are optional.

A motorized tonneau cover and an
integrated 60-quart cooler in the bed wall are optional.
Trims & Pricing

Cab, Off-Road & Technology
The Vulcan is offered exclusively as a five-passenger crew cab measuring 232.7 inches long on a
145.9-inch wheelbase. Standard four-corner air suspension provides 9.5 inches of ground clearance,
rising to 13.0 inches in the Cyclops's “Forge Crawl” off-road mode, with 33.4 inches of water-fording
depth. The Cyclops adds the “Vulcan Turn” tank-style tight-radius maneuver and a
0.5-degree-per-motor torque-vectoring system. Inside, a horizontal 18.5-inch touchscreen runs
Aether OS, and the truck includes the Guardian driver-assistance suite, a powered mid-gate that
opens to the cab, and a 14-speaker Helios audio system on the Cyclops. Towing range with a loaded
7,000-pound trailer is approximately 190 miles.
Warranty & Service Notes

The Alchemy Auto Codex  ·  Page 8
The Vulcan carries the standard 4-year / 50,000-mile bumper-to-bumper and 8-year / 100,000-mile
battery warranties and follows the EV maintenance schedule in Chapter 7. Because of its higher curb
weight and towing duty, Alchemy recommends a brake-system inspection every 15,000 miles for
owners who tow more than 5,000 pounds regularly, and a tire rotation every 6,000 miles when
running the optional 35-inch all-terrain tires.
Chapter 5: The Nimbus Hybrid SUV
The Nimbus is Alchemy Auto's three-row hybrid SUV and the brand's family flagship, governed
primarily by the Water pillar for comfort and the Earth pillar for durability. It seats up to seven and is
designed for owners who want Alchemy refinement with the long-range convenience of a hybrid. It is
assembled at the Flagstaff Atelier alongside the Aurelius.
Powertrain & Efficiency
The Nimbus pairs a 2.5-liter four-cylinder engine with two electric motors and a 1.8 kWh hybrid
battery for a combined 268 horsepower. A larger plug-in variant, the Nimbus Cumulus PHEV, uses
an 18.1 kWh battery to deliver 42 miles of all-electric range and a combined 302 horsepower. The
standard Nimbus hybrid is EPA-rated at 36 mpg combined (37 city / 34 highway), while the
Cumulus PHEV achieves 78 MPGe in electric-hybrid operation. Front-wheel drive is standard;
electronic all-wheel drive via a rear motor is a $2,200 option on all trims.
Seating, Cargo & Family Technology
The Nimbus offers three rows with seating for seven (an eight-seat bench is a no-cost option on lower
trims, and twin second-row captain's chairs are standard on the top trim). Cargo volume is 16.4 cubic
feet behind the third row, 41.8 cubic feet behind the second, and 84.6 cubic feet with both rear rows
folded.

Cargo volume is 16.4 cubic
feet behind the third row, 41.8 cubic feet behind the second, and 84.6 cubic feet with both rear rows
folded. The family technology suite, branded “Guardian,” includes standard automatic emergency
braking, adaptive cruise with lane centering, a rear-seat reminder, eight airbags, a 360-degree
camera, and a rear-seat intercom called “Cloud Call.” The Nimbus earned a 5-star overall safety
rating and offers built-in second- and third-row sunshades.

Dimensions, Towing & Colors
The Nimbus measures 203.4 inches long, 78.9 inches wide, and 70.1 inches tall on a 122.0-inch
wheelbase, with a curb weight of 4,560 pounds (4,910 for the Cumulus PHEV). It tows up to 3,500

The Alchemy Auto Codex  ·  Page 9
pounds in standard form and 5,000 pounds with the optional towing package and rear motor.
Standard colors are Albedo White, Quicksilver Silver, Storm Grey, and Nigredo Black, with Aurum
Gold available as a $1,200 premium finish. The Altus and Cumulus add a 14-speaker Helios system,
a panoramic fixed-glass roof, and a 12.3-inch digital instrument cluster paired with a 15.0-inch central
touchscreen running Aether OS.
Maintenance Notes
The Nimbus uses 0W-20 full-synthetic oil with a 4.6-quart capacity, changed every 10,000 miles or 12
months, and spark plugs at 60,000 miles. Its e-CVT is a sealed unit requiring no scheduled fluid
change. The 18.1 kWh battery in the Cumulus PHEV is covered by the 8-year / 100,000-mile
high-voltage warranty, and the standard hybrid's 1.8 kWh battery shares the same coverage.
Chapter 6: The Philosopher (Flagship)
The Philosopher is Alchemy Auto's halo car: a limited-run, all-electric grand tourer that represents the
fullest expression of the Transmutation philosophy and is dominated by the Air pillar. It is not a
volume product but a statement — the “Philosopher's Stone” made manifest in carbon and aluminum.
Only the most senior members of the College of Adepts are permitted to sign off on its build plaques.
Limited Run & Exclusivity
The Philosopher is strictly limited to 109 examples worldwide — a number chosen to honor the
founding year (2009) and the atomic number of an element yet to be named in the company's private
periodic table. Of these, 27 are reserved for North America. Each carries a sequential build number
engraved on a platinum medallion, and the first car, number 001, is retained permanently in the
Sedona museum and is never offered for sale.

Of these, 27 are reserved for North America. Each carries a sequential build number
engraved on a platinum medallion, and the first car, number 001, is retained permanently in the
Sedona museum and is never offered for sale.
Specifications
The Philosopher uses a tri-motor powertrain producing 1,114 horsepower and 1,050 lb-ft of torque,
reaching 60 mph in 1.99 seconds and a top speed of 217 mph. Its 118 kWh battery delivers an
EPA-estimated 405 miles of range and accepts up to 350 kW DC charging, achieving 10–80 percent
in 18 minutes. The body is full carbon fiber, holding curb weight to 4,420 pounds, and it rides on
active hydraulic suspension with carbon-ceramic brakes as standard. Each car is bespoke; there is a
single trim, simply called The Philosopher.
Allocation Process & Price
The Philosopher carries a base price of $498,000, before the typically extensive bespoke
commissioning, which averages an additional $85,000 per car. Allocation is by invitation only: a
prospective owner must hold Owner's Club “Adept” status (see Chapter 11), submit a written
commission letter to the College of Adepts, and place a fully refundable $50,000 reservation.
Allocations are confirmed in a single annual ceremony held each September at the Sedona
headquarters, and a non-resale covenant applies for the first 24 months of ownership.

The Alchemy Auto Codex  ·  Page 10
Bespoke Commissioning & Ownership
Every Philosopher is configured with a dedicated commissioning adept over a typical four-month
design process. Owners may specify paint from the unlimited “Quintessence” palette, choose
between three carbon-weave finishes, and commission a personal engraving on the platinum
medallion. A matching set of luggage and a 1:8 scale model of the owner's exact car are included.
Each Philosopher includes lifetime complimentary scheduled maintenance, the full Adept tier of the
Owner's Club (see Chapter 11), an 8-year / 120,000-mile battery warranty, and white-glove annual
collection for service anywhere in the continental United States. Production is capped at 24 cars per
year to preserve the hand-built character and the 109-unit lifetime ceiling.
Chapter 7: Maintenance & Service Schedules
Alchemy Auto vehicles are engineered for long intervals, but adherence to the published schedule is
a condition of full warranty coverage. Service may be performed at any authorized Alchemy Atelier or
by the mobile “Quintessence” service vans. All intervals below are stated as whichever comes first —
mileage or time.
EV Models (Aurelius, Vulcan, Philosopher)
Electric models follow a simplified schedule. Rotate tires every 7,500 miles or 6 months. Replace the
cabin air filter every 24,000 miles or 24 months. Inspect and, if needed, replace the brake fluid every
3 years regardless of mileage. Replace the battery coolant at 100,000 miles and again every 50,000
miles thereafter. Service the air-conditioning desiccant at 6 years. The high-voltage battery itself
requires no scheduled service, but a software-driven health check is performed at every visit. There is
no engine oil, spark plug, or transmission-fluid service on EV models.

Service the air-conditioning desiccant at 6 years. The high-voltage battery itself
requires no scheduled service, but a software-driven health check is performed at every visit. There is
no engine oil, spark plug, or transmission-fluid service on EV models.
Hybrid & Petrol Models (Mercurial GT, Nimbus)
For the Mercurial GT and Nimbus, change the engine oil and filter every 10,000 miles or 12 months
using 0W-20 full-synthetic oil (the Mercurial GT requires 5W-40 and a 7.4-quart capacity; the Nimbus
takes 4.6 quarts). Replace the engine air filter every 30,000 miles. Replace spark plugs at 60,000
miles on the Nimbus and 45,000 miles on the turbocharged Mercurial GT. Replace the cabin air filter
every 15,000 miles. Flush engine coolant at 100,000 miles, then every 50,000 miles. The Mercurial
GT's dual-clutch transmission fluid is replaced at 60,000 miles; the Nimbus hybrid uses a sealed
e-CVT requiring no scheduled fluid change.

Chapter 8: Warranty & Protection Plans
Every new Alchemy Auto vehicle is covered by a layered factory warranty, all transferable to
subsequent owners at no cost. Coverage begins on the date of original delivery.
Factory Coverage
The bumper-to-bumper (New Vehicle Limited) warranty runs 4 years or 50,000 miles, whichever
comes first. The powertrain warranty runs 6 years or 70,000 miles. The high-voltage battery and
electric drive components are covered for 8 years or 100,000 miles, whichever comes first,
with a guarantee against capacity loss below 70 percent of original rated capacity in that period. The
Philosopher receives an extended 8-year / 120,000-mile battery warranty. Corrosion (perforation)
coverage runs 12 years with unlimited mileage. Complimentary 24-hour roadside assistance is
included for 5 years with unlimited mileage and covers towing to the nearest Atelier, lockout service,
and EV flatbed transport with up to 50 miles of charge delivery.
Extended Protection Tiers
Three extended service contracts may be purchased before the factory bumper-to-bumper warranty
expires. The Copper Plan extends component coverage to 7 years / 90,000 miles for $1,850. The
Silver Plan extends to 8 years / 120,000 miles and adds wear items for $3,200. The Gold Plan
extends to 10 years / 150,000 miles, adds wear items, cosmetic wheel and tire coverage, and key
replacement, for $4,950. All tiers include a $0 deductible at any Alchemy Atelier ($100 deductible at
third-party shops) and are fully transferable.

All tiers include a $0 deductible at any Alchemy Atelier ($100 deductible at
third-party shops) and are fully transferable.

Chapter 9: EV Charging & Battery Care
All electric Alchemy Auto models — the Aurelius, the Vulcan, the Philosopher, and the plug-in Nimbus
Cumulus — use the North American Charging Standard (NACS) connector for both AC and DC
charging, and ship with a CCS adapter at no charge.
Connector Types & Charge Rates
AC charging (Level 1, 120 V) adds roughly 3–4 miles of range per hour and is intended only for
emergencies. Level 2 AC (240 V) charges at up to 11 kW on the Aurelius and Nimbus and up to 11.5
kW on the Vulcan and Philosopher, replenishing a typical battery overnight. DC fast charging peaks
at 210 kW (Aurelius LFP), 250 kW (Aurelius Magnum Opus), 270 kW (Vulcan), and 350 kW
(Philosopher).

Battery Longevity & Cold-Weather Guidance
Alchemy Auto recommends keeping the battery between 20 and 80 percent for daily use and
charging to 100 percent only before long trips. LFP-equipped Aurelius models are an exception:
owners should charge LFP packs to 100 percent at least once per week to keep the state-of-charge
estimate calibrated. Avoid leaving the vehicle at very high or very low charge for extended periods. In
cold weather, use the scheduled-departure preconditioning feature, which warms the battery while
still plugged in; charging speeds below 32°F (0°C) may be reduced by up to 40 percent until the
battery reaches its optimal 68°F (20°C) operating temperature.

cold weather, use the scheduled-departure preconditioning feature, which warms the battery while
still plugged in; charging speeds below 32°F (0°C) may be reduced by up to 40 percent until the
battery reaches its optimal 68°F (20°C) operating temperature. Heat is managed by a liquid thermal
system that activates automatically.
The Home-Charger Program
Through the “Hearth” home-charger program, every new EV buyer receives a complimentary
Alchemy Level 2 home wall connector (a $650 value) rated at 11.5 kW / 48 amps. Buyers may
instead elect a $500 public-charging credit. Professional installation is offered at a flat $1,200 (typical)
through certified electricians, and Owner's Club members at the Adept tier receive installation at no
charge. The Hearth connector supports scheduled charging, load sharing across two vehicles, and
integration with home solar.

The Alchemy Auto Codex  ·  Page 13
Chapter 10: Financing, Leasing & Incentives
Alchemy Financial Services (AFS) is the captive lender for all Alchemy Auto purchases and leases.
Rates and terms below are illustrative of the Spring 2026 program and are subject to credit approval.
Purchase APR Tiers
Financing is offered in three credit tiers. Tier 1 (Aurum), for credit scores of 740 and above,
qualifies for rates as low as 3.9 percent APR on 60-month terms. Tier 2 (Argent), for scores from
680 to 739, starts at 5.9 percent APR. Tier 3 (Cuprum), for scores from 620 to 679, starts at 8.4
percent APR. Standard terms run from 36 to 84 months; AFS recommends a down payment of at
least 15 percent of the purchase price to optimize the rate and limit negative equity.

Leasing
Leases are quoted using a money factor rather than an APR. A representative Spring 2026 Aurelius
Lumen lease uses a money factor of 0.00125 (equivalent to roughly 3.0 percent APR) with a
36-month / 12,000-mile-per-year term and a 58 percent residual value. For example, an Aurelius
Lumen with $4,500 due at signing leases for an estimated $749 per month. Excess mileage is billed
at $0.30 per mile, and lessees enjoy a complimentary pull-ahead waiver of the final three payments
when leasing or financing a new Alchemy vehicle.
Incentives, Loyalty & Trade-In
Eligible EV models may qualify for the federal clean-vehicle tax credit of up to $7,500, subject to
buyer income and vehicle assembly requirements; AFS can apply the credit at the point of sale.

Incentives, Loyalty & Trade-In
Eligible EV models may qualify for the federal clean-vehicle tax credit of up to $7,500, subject to
buyer income and vehicle assembly requirements; AFS can apply the credit at the point of sale. The
“Transmutation Loyalty” program grants returning owners a $1,500 credit toward their next
Alchemy vehicle and a 0.5 percentage-point APR reduction. The trade-in process is handled through
the “Crucible” appraisal tool: an owner submits vehicle details online, receives a firm written offer
valid for 7 days or 500 miles, and may apply the value directly to a new purchase to reduce sales tax
in eligible states.
Chapter 11: The Aurelius Owner's Club &
Concierge Services
Ownership of any Alchemy Auto vehicle confers automatic membership in the Aurelius Owner's Club,
the brand's community and concierge organization. Despite its name, the Club welcomes owners of

The Alchemy Auto Codex  ·  Page 14
all five models; it takes its name from the first production model. Membership is administered through
the “Magnus” concierge application and the brand's AI concierge of the same name.
Membership Tiers
There are three tiers. Initiate is automatic and complimentary for every owner; it includes the digital
concierge, over-the-air feature updates, and access to the member forums. Artisan ($600 per year,
or complimentary for owners of two or more Alchemy vehicles) adds priority service scheduling, the
loaner-vehicle guarantee, and quarterly detailing. Adept (by invitation, or automatic for Philosopher
owners) is the highest tier; it includes everything in Artisan plus complimentary valet pickup and
delivery, free Hearth home-charger installation, eligibility for Philosopher allocation, and access to the
Philosopher's Lounge.

Valet Service & The Philosopher's Lounge
The concierge valet service, available to Artisan and Adept members, collects a vehicle from the
owner's home or office for scheduled service and returns it the same day with a complimentary detail;
Adept members receive this at no charge within a 60-mile radius of any Atelier. The Philosopher's
Lounge is a network of private owner spaces — the flagship located within the Sedona headquarters
— offering a quiet workspace, a tasting room, charging while owners wait, and a curated library on
the history of metallurgy and design. Lounge access is reserved for Adept members and any
Philosopher owner.
Annual Events
The Club calendar centers on three annual gatherings. The Rubedo Rally, held each October in
Sedona, is a two-day driving event culminating in the Philosopher allocation ceremony. The Solstice
Salon, in December, previews the coming model year and unveils new colors and software.

The Rubedo Rally, held each October in
Sedona, is a two-day driving event culminating in the Philosopher allocation ceremony. The Solstice
Salon, in December, previews the coming model year and unveils new colors and software. The
Founder's Day Concours, held every March 14 to mark the 2009 founding, gathers owners'
best-kept examples for judging, with the winner's vehicle displayed for one year in the Sedona
museum beside Philosopher number 001. Members at all tiers may attend Founder's Day; the
Rubedo Rally is reserved for Artisan and Adept members.
